reviews.review_description:
I did this as a remote work team building exercise, and it's great! Don't mind the 1-star review, they didn't understand that this is a scheduled experience, with a live host. Book a time at their website and then log into the app at the appropriate time and enter your code. It's far more than a lobby!

Pro-tip: be very nice to the host for extra rewards and fun.
Thank you so much for the experience

This show was amazing. The ability to interact with a live actor rather than a NPC adds so much to the experience. You can cheer on your team, insult the villain, and solve puzzles as a group.

I see other reviews complaining that the experience is $75, but it’s not. It’s $75 for a group of EIGHT which is only 9 bucks per person! The experience was AMAZING and honestly should be more expensive based on the quality of the performance.
The live hosts are amazing. This is the future of virtual reality. So funny, so fun, and every show is unique just for you! Great gameplay, good job all around!
Super fun way to spend time with friends. The performer was hilarious. Not actually sure if the dog and dr Crumb are the same person still

reviews.review_title: Best experience I’ve had on Oculus
reviews.review_description:
I loved this experience. I got to meet up with friends and we dove into some legit VR puzzle solving (think escape room with punching gloves) with live actors guiding me thorough the process.

I smiled so much my cheeks hurt by the end.  I was totally lost in this world, what a great escape.

Highly recommend.
Great for friends and family. Gamers and non gamers alike can enjoy this one.

What is the room key?
What numbers its?? I dont undertand?!!
I mean, it is a game, but it's also a really fun social experience starring a LIVE ACTOR. Unlike some of the VR theater I've experienced, getting started was easy and intuitive.
A waste of space, especially since you cannot permanently remove an app's icon from the quest. It's only a lobby with a $75 a game price tag. Quite a cash grab.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. We've learned that we need to do a better job of explaining that our experience is actually more like a live performance/escape room with a live game master. We've also learned a lot from the community that we need to rethink how our next experience can be something you play at all times and then have ticketed events inside the game.
